title: Fix admin panel login 'Service unavailable' and restore working admin authentication

requirements.text: Fix backend admin authentication so that calling `adminLogin(password)` accepts the exact admin password `JATINSHARMA2580` (ignoring accidental leading/trailing whitespace in the submitted password), returns a non-empty admin session token on success, and returns an explicit invalid-password result when the password is incorrect.

quotes:
Admin panel mein service unavailable please try again later
Only bus yahi dikkat a rahi hai admin panel login nahin ho raha hai sirf admin pahle inko check karo aur yah problem theek kar do bus

acceptanceCriteria: When `adminLogin("JATINSHARMA2580")` is called, the backend returns a success variant that contains a non-empty token string.
When `adminLogin("  JATINSHARMA2580  ")` is called, the backend returns the same success behavior (whitespace trimmed).
When `adminLogin("wrong")` is called, the backend returns an explicit invalid-password variant (not a trap).

requirements.text: Ensure the backend exposes the admin session APIs expected by the frontend contract and that they behave consistently: `adminLogin(password)` for issuing tokens and `validateAdminSession(token)` for validating them. A token returned from `adminLogin` must be accepted by `validateAdminSession` until it expires, and validation must refresh the token’s last-seen timestamp according to the backend’s session timeout rules.

quotes:
Admin panel mein service unavailable please try again later
Only bus yahi dikkat a rahi hai admin panel login nahin ho raha hai sirf admin pahle inko check karo aur yah problem theek kar do bus

acceptanceCriteria: `validateAdminSession(token)` returns `true` for a freshly issued admin token, and `false` for an unknown token.
Calling `validateAdminSession(token)` updates the token’s timestamp so the session remains valid while being periodically validated within the timeout window.
After exceeding the configured session timeout without validation, `validateAdminSession(token)` returns `false` and the token is removed/treated as expired.

requirements.text: Make the admin password value persist correctly across canister upgrades so upgrades do not revert/retain an unintended previous admin password. If upgrade handling is required under the project migration policy, add/adjust the backend upgrade/migration logic to preserve existing state while ensuring the stored admin password is set to `JATINSHARMA2580` after upgrade.

quotes: admin panel login nahin ho raha hai sirf admin pahle inko check karo aur yah problem theek kar do bus

acceptanceCriteria: After an upgrade, the admin password used by `adminLogin` remains correct and does not unexpectedly change due to initialization defaults.
If migration logic is applied, it does not wipe other existing in-canister state (e.g., clients/shipments/invoices) and the admin password is `JATINSHARMA2580` after upgrade.

requirements.text: Verify the admin login flow works end-to-end in the UI using the existing `AdminLoginCard` + `useAdminSession` behavior: entering the correct password successfully authenticates and shows the admin dashboard; entering an incorrect password shows exactly "Invalid password. Please try again."; backend/method/candid failures show a service-unavailable style message in English.

quotes: Admin panel mein service unavailable please try again later

acceptanceCriteria: With a working backend, entering the correct password results in the admin session token being stored in `localStorage` under `admin_session_token` and the UI transitions into the authenticated admin experience.
Entering a wrong password shows exactly: "Invalid password. Please try again."
If the backend is unreachable or the admin methods are missing/mismatched, the UI displays: "Service unavailable. Please try again later." (English-only).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in `backend/main.mo` (no additional backend services).
Do not edit any frontend files under `frontend/src/components/ui` or the immutable hook paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Adding new authentication providers (e.g., Internet Identity admin login) beyond the existing password-based admin login.
Implementing real-time features or external databases/services.
Changing client (non-admin) authentication flows unless required to fix the admin login regression.
